SEARCH

Class WeightedElement<E,W extends Comparable<W>>

java.lang.Object
WeightedElement<E,W>
Type Parameters:
E - the type of the element stored
W - the type of the weight, must itself be Comparable
All Implemented Interfaces:
Comparable<WeightedElement<E,W>>
public class WeightedElement<E,W extends Comparable<W>> extends Object implements Comparable<WeightedElement<E,W>>
A generic container pairing an element of type E with a weight of type W. W must be Comparable so that two WeightedElements can be ordered by weight. This class implements Comparable by delegating entirely to W's compareTo method.
Version:
4/28
Author:
Ali Kablanbek

  • Constructor Summary

    Description
    WeightedElement(E element, W weight)
    Constructs a WeightedElement with the given element and weight.

    int

    Two WeightedElements are equal when both their element and weight are equal.

    Returns the element stored in this WeightedElement.

  • Constructor Details

    • WeightedElement

      public WeightedElement(E element, W weight)
      Constructs a WeightedElement with the given element and weight.
      Parameters:
      element - the element to store
      weight - the weight associated with this element

  • Method Details

    • getElement

      public E getElement()
      Returns the element stored in this WeightedElement.
      Returns:
      the element

    • getWeight

      public W getWeight()
      Returns the weight stored in this WeightedElement.
      Returns:
      the weight

    • compareTo

      public int compareTo(WeightedElement<E,W> other)
      Compares this WeightedElement to another by weight only. Relies on W's compareTo so that the ordering matches W's natural ordering.
      Specified by:
      compareTo in interface Comparable<E>
      Parameters:
      other - the other WeightedElement to compare against
      Returns:
      a negative integer if this weight is less than other's weight, zero if equal, a positive integer if greater

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object obj)
      Two WeightedElements are equal when both their element and weight are equal. This is needed for correct HashMap key behavior in MyClassicPriorityQueue.
      Overrides:
      equals in class Object

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Hash code is derived from both element and weight to be consistent with equals.
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a human-readable string in the form (element, weight).
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
